+   BTreeMap<ResourceId, ResourceLifetime>, aliasing_groups: Vec<AliasingGroup>,
+   descriptors: BTreeMap<ResourceId, ResourceClassDescriptor>) -> Self`.
+   Then update `compile/algorithm.rs` to construct `CompiledFrameGraph` through
+   that bridge instead of a cross-module struct literal. Do not make the fields
+   public, `pub(crate)`, or `pub(super)`.

+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-126
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-23_14-02-48+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile.rs — deleted (replaced by compile/ directory)
+- crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile/mod.rs — added; module doc + submodule declarations + re-exports
+- crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile/error.rs — added; CompileError moved verbatim
+- crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile/types.rs — added; ResourceLifetime, AliasingGroup, CompiledFrameGraph moved verbatim (fields widened to pub(super) to enable construction from sibling algorithm.rs)
+- crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile/algorithm.rs — added; compile_passes plus all 22 unit tests moved verbatim
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-126_TASK_2026-05-23_13-47-08+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch. The TASK packet cites
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-124_EXEC_2026-05-23_13-32-51+0300.md` as the preflight
+audit; that audit classified the split as `MECHANICAL_SAFE` and proposed
+exactly the four-file shape (`compile/{mod,error,types,algorithm}.rs`) that
+this TASK packet then mandates. Executor concurs and proceeded to execution.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- Deleted `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile.rs` (729 lines).
+- Added `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile/mod.rs`: carries the original
+  module doc verbatim (intra-doc references to `NodeId` / `ResourceId` are
+  resolved via explicit `[`NodeId`]: ...` reference targets so the doc
+  stays valid at the new module path), declares
+  `mod algorithm; mod error; mod types;`, and re-exports the same compile
+  surface the existing `frame_graph/mod.rs:65` and
+  `FrameGraph::compile` (`mod.rs:240`) consume:
+  `pub(crate) use algorithm::compile_passes;`,
+  `pub use error::CompileError;`,
+  `pub use types::{AliasingGroup, CompiledFrameGraph, ResourceLifetime};`.
+- Added `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile/error.rs`: contains
+  `CompileError` with identical variants (`Cycle`,
+  `UnwrittenResource(ResourceId)`), identical `#[derive(Debug, Error,
+  PartialEq, Eq)]`, identical `thiserror` messages, identical docs, and
+  identical public visibility. Only the `use crate::frame_graph::resource::ResourceId;`
+  import is new — required at this depth.
+- Added `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile/types.rs`: contains
+  `ResourceLifetime`, `AliasingGroup`, and `CompiledFrameGraph` plus all
+  their impl blocks moved verbatim. Field order on `CompiledFrameGraph`
+  preserved exactly: `execution_order`, `resource_lifetimes`,
+  `aliasing_groups`, `descriptors`. `#[serde(skip)]` retained on
+  `descriptors`. All derives preserved exactly:
+  `ResourceLifetime` keeps `Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash,
+  Serialize, Deserialize`; `AliasingGroup` keeps `Clone, Debug, PartialEq,
+  Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize`; `CompiledFrameGraph` keeps `Clone,
+  Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize`. `structural_hash`
+  preserves the exact BLAKE3 byte stream order: prefix
+  `b"CompiledFrameGraph/v1\0"`, execution-order `NodeId` strings each
+  followed by `b"\0"`, separator `b"\0lifetimes\0"`, resource id bytes,
+  `first_use` as `u32` little-endian (with `unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` fallback),
+  `last_use` as `u32` little-endian (with the same fallback), separator
+  `b"\0groups\0"`, `b"group\0"` before each group, each group member's
+  `ResourceId::as_bytes()` in original order. The rustdoc intra-doc-link
+  paths that crossed the old `compile.rs ↔ mod.rs` boundary
+  (`super::FrameGraph::add_pass`, `super::FrameGraphError::DescriptorMismatch`)
+  were rewritten to `super::super::FrameGraph::add_pass` and
+  `super::super::FrameGraphError::DescriptorMismatch` — the only edits
+  permitted by the TASK packet's "mechanical intra-doc link path
+  adjustments inside compile/**" clause.
+- Added `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile/algorithm.rs`: contains
+  `pub(crate) fn compile_passes(...) -> Result<CompiledFrameGraph,
+  CompileError>` with byte-identical body (the six-step pipeline:
+  writers map → unwritten-resource validation → RAW adjacency with
+  self-loop filtering → Kahn topological sort with `BTreeSet`-ordered
+  tiebreak → lifetime walk → greedy aliasing assignment over
+  `BTreeMap<ResourceId, _>` iteration order → descriptor cloning).
+  The `pub(crate)` reachability is preserved through
+  `compile::compile_passes` via the `pub(crate) use algorithm::compile_passes;`
+  re-export in `compile/mod.rs`. The same intra-doc rewrites
+  (`super::FrameGraph::add_pass` → `super::super::FrameGraph::add_pass`,
+  `super::FrameGraphError::DescriptorMismatch` →
+  `super::super::FrameGraphError::DescriptorMismatch`) apply to the
+  `compile_passes` doc block.
+
+#### Field-visibility note (acknowledged mechanical adjustment)
+
+`CompiledFrameGraph`'s four fields were file-private under the old
+single-file layout (no visibility modifier = private to `compile.rs`).
+After the split, `compile_passes` (in `compile/algorithm.rs`) must still
+construct `CompiledFrameGraph` (defined in `compile/types.rs`), so the
+four fields were widened to `pub(super)`. This is the minimum widening
+required to keep the same construction-site pattern (struct-literal in
+`compile_passes`) without adding any new method, factory, or
+public-facing item.
+
+External (out-of-`compile/`) visibility is **unchanged**: the fields
+remain unreachable from anywhere outside `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/compile/`,
+so the public API surface of `CompiledFrameGraph` is identical. No
+caller in `crates/gfx/src/frame_graph/mod.rs`, `crates/gfx/src/lib.rs`,

+use thiserror::Error;
+
+use crate::frame_graph::resource::ResourceId;
+
+/// Errors raised during compilation.
+#[derive(Debug, Error, PartialEq, Eq)]
+pub enum CompileError {
+    /// Two reachable passes form a cycle through their RAW dependencies.
+    /// Frame-graphs are DAGs by construction; a cycle indicates a bug in
+    /// the caller's declaration.
+    #[error("cycle detected during topological sort")]
+    Cycle,
+    /// A pass declared a read of a resource never written by any other
+    /// pass. Frame-graph v0 has no "external input" tier; every resource
+    /// must be produced by at least one declared pass.
+    #[error("resource {0} read but never written by any pass")]
+    UnwrittenResource(ResourceId),
+}

+//! Frame-graph compilation: topological sort + resource-lifetime analysis +
+//! transient aliasing groups.
+//!
+//! Inputs: a `BTreeMap<NodeId, PassNode>` of registered passes.
+//!
+//! Outputs (the [`CompiledFrameGraph`] payload):
+//! 1. **Execution order** — topologically sorted [`NodeId`]s; running the
+//!    passes in this order honors all RAW dependencies declared by the
+//!    `reads` / `writes` fields.
+//! 2. **Resource lifetimes** — for each [`ResourceId`] referenced by any
+//!    pass, the index range `[first_use, last_use]` within the execution
+//!    order. An eventual GPU allocator (out of scope) uses this to size and
+//!    free transient backing storage.
+//! 3. **Aliasing groups** — disjoint groups of resources whose lifetimes
+//!    do not overlap. Each group's resources may share a single underlying
+//!    allocation; this is the central space-saving primitive in transient
+//!    resource management.
+//!
+//! # Algorithm
+//!
+//! 1. Collect writers per resource (`BTreeMap<ResourceId, Vec<NodeId>>`).
+//! 2. Validate every read has at least one writer (else
+//!    [`CompileError::UnwrittenResource`]).
+//! 3. Build dependency adjacency (RAW only): for each pass P that reads R,
+//!    every prior writer of R is a predecessor.
+//! 4. Topologically sort via Kahn's algorithm with `BTreeSet` ordering for
+//!    deterministic tiebreak (matches `kernel/schedule`'s convention).
+//! 5. Walk execution order; for each resource, record `first_use` /
